title: Europe’s New German Question
gist: Marco Buti and Francesco Nicoli warn that without an EU-level framework to pool defense resources into real capability, higher national spending — especially Germany going it alone — will just perpetuate Europe's fragmented, outdated defense system and its 70-year dependence on the United States.

insights.claim: Without an EU-level framework capable of converting national resources into real scale, Europe's defense capabilities will stay limited no matter how much individual member countries spend.
insights.speaker: Marco Buti and Francesco Nicoli
insights.so_what: Argues that European defense-spending increases will not translate into capability gains absent EU-level pooling — relevant to how investors should read headline European defense-budget figures versus actual deterrence capacity.
insights.quote: Without a EU-level framework capable of turning resources into scale, the bloc’s defense capabilities will remain limited, no matter how much member countries spends

insights.claim: If Germany in particular insists on building up its defense capabilities in isolation rather than through EU cooperation, Europe could remain fully dependent on the US for key military assets.
insights.speaker: Marco Buti and Francesco Nicoli
insights.so_what: Names Germany's go-it-alone posture as the specific risk factor for continued European strategic dependence on the US, a variable to track in EU defense-industrial and NATO-burden-sharing positioning.
insights.quote: If Germany, in particular, insists on going it alone, Europe could remain fully dependent on the US for key assets

insights.claim: Many EU countries, starting with Germany, assume higher defense spending will automatically generate the capabilities needed to keep Europe secure, but pursuing that spending in isolation would merely perpetuate the fragmented, outdated defense system that has left Europe dependent on the US for more than 70 years.
insights.speaker: Marco Buti and Francesco Nicoli
insights.so_what: Quantifies the duration of the dependency problem (70+ years) and identifies the assumption-error driving current national-level defense-spending debates in the EU.
insights.quote: will merely perpetuate the fragmented, outdated defense system that has left them dependent on the United States for more than 70 years
insights.locator: FLORENCE/TURIN—Many European Union countries, starting with Germany
